name: Lightfoot Wines
signature: A small Main Ridge estate with a tight Pinot and Chardonnay range and one of the ridge's most peaceful tasting rooms
editorNote:
Lightfoot is the kind of Main Ridge cellar door that rewards a quiet afternoon visit rather than a Saturday long-weekend one. The estate is small, the tasting room is unhurried, and the range is deliberately focused: Chardonnay, Pinot Noir, sometimes a Rosé or a sparkling, all made from the surrounding blocks with an obvious restraint and the kind of house style that feels consistent from year to year.

This is an underrated Main Ridge stop for visitors who have already worked through the bigger names on the ridge and want a quieter counterpoint. The cellar door is intimate enough that the person pouring can walk you through the choices in a way the bigger estates' tasting counters rarely allow.

Book ahead. Best visited mid-week when the ridge is quieter. Ask about current allocations.

heroImage.alt: Peninsula vineyard landscape evocative of Lightfoot Wines, Mornington Peninsula wine country
